name: Yelp detect-secrets Secret Scanner
what_it_does: Open-source Python library and CLI for detecting secrets (API keys, passwords, tokens) in codebases — enabling developers and security teams to prevent accidental credential commits through pre-commit hook integration, baseline file management for tracking known false positives, CI/CD scanning, and a plugin-based detection architecture supporting AWS keys, Slack tokens, private keys, JWT tokens, base64-encoded secrets, and custom patterns.
use_cases: Preventing secret commits via detect-secrets pre-commit hook integration for developer workflows
Scanning codebases for accidentally committed secrets via detect-secrets CLI for security audits
Managing false positives via detect-secrets baseline file for team-wide secret scanning configuration
Integrating secret detection via detect-secrets in CI/CD pipelines for automated policy enforcement
Writing custom secret detection plugins via detect-secrets for organization-specific token patterns
not_for: Runtime secret rotation or vault management (use HashiCorp Vault for secret management)
Historical git commit scanning beyond current state (use gitleaks or trufflehog for git history)
SaaS-managed secret scanning with dashboard (use GitGuardian for team-managed scanning)
best_when: A development team wants lightweight, customizable secret detection integrated into pre-commit hooks and CI/CD — particularly where false-positive management via baseline files is important for developer experience.
avoid_when: You need full git history scanning, SaaS dashboard, or enterprise managed secret detection — detect-secrets is a developer-focused CLI tool without a centralized service.

auth.notes: No authentication required. CLI tool and Python library — no API service. Run locally: detect-secrets scan or detect-secrets audit. Pre-commit integration via .pre-commit-config.yaml. No network calls for detection (offline). Python importable library for custom integrations. No webhooks. No REST API.

pricing.notes: Created by Yelp (San Francisco). Open source: Apache 2.0. GitHub: Yelp/detect-secrets (3,000+ stars). Python package (pip install detect-secrets). Plugin architecture: built-in plugins for AWS, Slack, private keys, Stripe, Twilio, JWT, hex high entropy, base64 high entropy, keyword detection. Baseline file (.secrets.baseline): JSON file tracking known secrets to allow (false positives marked as not-real). pre-commit.ci integration via pre-commit hooks. CI integration: detect-secrets-hook command for pipeline use. Custom plugins: subclass BasePlugin. Companion to gitleaks/trufflehog for comprehensive secret scanning strategy.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: CLI tool only — no API. Apache 2.0. Python library for programmatic use. No auth. Baseline file for FP management. pre-commit integration. Offline scanning. Yelp open source.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Scanning is deterministic — same codebase always produces same results. Baseline audit decisions persist in .secrets.baseline JSON file. Fully idempotent for same input.
known_agent_gotchas: No REST API — this is a local CLI tool and Python library, not a network service
Baseline file (.secrets.baseline) must be committed to repo and kept up-to-date
detect-secrets scan > .secrets.baseline to create baseline; detect-secrets audit to review
High entropy detection produces false positives for non-secret high-entropy strings
Does NOT scan git history — only scans current working tree state (use gitleaks for history)
pre-commit hook: detect-secrets-hook -- .secrets.baseline fails if new secrets found

security_score_components.security_notes: No credentials required — zero auth attack surface. Apache 2.0 open source — fully auditable. Offline operation — no secret data leaves developer machine. Designed specifically to prevent secret exposure. Baseline file management allows team coordination without false positive noise. Python-based — auditable codebase. User-controlled: runs on developer or CI infrastructure only.
